articles.title: Thousands gather to see Premier League winners
articles.description: Thousands of fans gathered in North London to see Arsenal men celebrate their Premier League title and the women's team's Champions League success with an open-top bus parade.

articles.title: Arsenal 'pain' will fuel fire after Champions League heartbreak
articles.description: On Sunday, Arsenal will get on an open bus parade to celebrate their Premier League title less than 24 hours after their Champions League final heartbreak.

articles.description: Gabriel's first penalty for Arsenal ends in heartache as his shootout miss hands Paris St-Germain a second successive Champions League title.

articles.description: Arsenal fans reacted outside the Emirates Stadium after seeing their side lose on penalties to PSG in the Champions League final.

articles.description: Arsenal Fan TV's Robbie Lyle says he would give up all his online followers to see his club win the Champions League against Paris Saint-Germain on Saturday.

articles.description: Police in Budapest have made arrests and are studying CCTV footage to identify supporters fighting before Saturday's Champions League final between Arsenal and Paris St-Germain.

articles.description: Aaron Ramsey describes Mikel Arteta as an inspiration as he aims to follow his former Arsenal team-mate's success.

articles.description: BBC Sport's tactics correspondent Umir Irfan looks at how Arsenal can defeat Paris St-Germain in Saturday's Champions League final.

articles.title: Arsenal's Timber fit to start Champions League final
articles.description: Mikel Arteta says that Dutch defender Jurrien Timber is fit to start the Champions League final after more than two months out with a groin injury.

articles.title: Saka, Odegaard and messages from legends
articles.description: Club legends Thierry Henry and Patrick Viera have wished Arsenal players Bukayo Saka and Martin Odegaard good luck ahead of the Champions League final.

articles.description: Fans will need a subscription to watch the Champions League final between Arsenal and PSG after TNT Sports decides against making the match free to air.

articles.description: Twelve years ago Arsenal keeper David Raya made his debut for non-league Southport; on Saturday he plays in the Champions League final - this is how he got there.

articles.title: Paris Saint-Germain retain Champions League as Arsenal dream dashed in shootout
articles.description: It was a showpiece that held the football world in its grip, the tension mounting exponentially, everything on the line. For Paris Saint-Germain, there was the opportunity to make it clear that this is a dynastic team; the rarity of retaining a Champions League title. For Arsenal, it was simple. Never mind the Invincibles. They stood to be immortal, a first victory in this competition to follow th

articles.title: When Arsenal beat PSG on their way to winning the Cup Winners’ Cup
articles.description: Arsenal relied on their brilliant defence to see off PSG in the semi-finals and frustrate Parma in the final in 1994 By That 1980s Sports Blog The Cup Winners’ Cup became a victim of the Champions League’s expansion in the late 1990s, but there could be no questioning the quality of the competition when Arsenal won it in the 1993-94 season. articles.title: More than 150 million people will watch tonight’s Champions League final. It’s PSG v Arsenal – and most of Africa | Sean Jacobs
articles.description: Many teams have fans abroad, but the bond between the north London club and ordinary Africans is on a different level. A continent expects If Arsenal win the Champions League final later today, expect euphoria across Africa. articles.title: My Arsenal devotion began with watching them lose in a South African cinema
articles.description: As a boy in the apartheid era I saw footage of the Gunners beaten in the 1969 League Cup final – on Saturday I will attend the Champions League showpiece with my son I fell for Arsenal in the white‑and-black world of apartheid, where television was banned as a tool of communist propaganda and the club of my dreams was 6,000 miles away and mostly invisible to me. articles.title: Arsenal fans use ‘trains, planes and automobiles’ in race to Budapest for Champions League final
articles.description: As flights and hotels sell out and prices soar, supporters go to great lengths to see Gunners face Paris Saint-Germain Since Arsenal’s euphoric Premier League win last week, it has been a never-ending party in much of north London (apart from in Tottenham, although they had their own cause for celebration ). articles.title: Sabalenka vs Osaka to play first French Open women's night match for three years
articles.description: The French Open will stage a women's match in its night session for the first time in three years when a pair of four-time Grand Slam champions, Aryna Sabalenka and Naomi Osaka, meet on Monday.

articles.title: Swiatek suffers shock French Open loss to Kostyuk
articles.description: World No 3 Iga Swiatek crashed out of the French Open with a 7-5 6-1 straight-sets loss to Marta Kostyuk in the fourth round.

articles.title: Osaka sparkles again to set up Sabalenka clash at French Open
articles.description: Naomi Osaka once again lit up Roland-Garros with her choice of outfit as well as her talent on court as she beat American teenager Iva Jovic to set up a meeting with world No 1 Aryna Sabalenka in the fourth round of the French Open.

articles.title: Ukraine's Oliynykova condemns 'evil' war after loss to Russia's Shnaider
articles.description: Oleksandra Oliynykova gave an impassioned plea for tennis to stand up against the "evil" war in Ukraine after losing to Russia's Diana Shnaider in the third round of the French Open.

articles.title: Serrano stops Hanson in second round, Han beats Holm by decision in rematch
articles.description: Amanda Serrano retained her unified featherweight world title and tied the all-time knockout record with a barnstorming second-round stoppage victory over Cheyenne Hanson in the MVP Women's co-main event.

articles.title: Wembanyama's Spurs dethrone Thunder to reach NBA Finals
articles.description: Victor Wembanyama and the San Antonio Spurs secured a thrilling 111-103 win in Oklahoma City to dethrone the defending champions and secure a place in the NBA Finals.
